html:
GAF Commercial Product Lines and Spec Considerations

GAF's commercial roofing division produces TPO membrane and modified bitumen products used across a range of Spartanburg County building types, from strip retail reroofs to larger industrial applications. This page covers the factual product and warranty details relevant to specifying GAF materials, without implying any certification or partnership beyond installing according to published manufacturer guidelines.

Product Categories

GAF's commercial catalog includes TPO membrane in multiple thicknesses and reinforcement configurations, along with modified bitumen products for multi-ply applications, plus associated insulation and accessory lines sold as part of complete assemblies. As with any manufacturer, product formulations and available options are updated periodically, so we confirm the current data sheet for the exact product being specified rather than working from memory of prior projects. GAF also offers roofing accessory products, including edge metal, walkway pads, and pipe boot flashing, positioned to complete a full system spec from a single manufacturer, which simplifies warranty accountability compared to sourcing accessories from multiple suppliers.

TPO Spec Sheet Details

GAF's TPO data sheets list membrane thickness, reinforcement scrim weight, tensile and tear strength, and relevant ASTM test references for each product variant. On buildings near the I-85 distribution corridor where we're weighing 60-mil against 80-mil thickness, those published figures are what we use to walk owners through the actual performance difference rather than relying on general thickness assumptions that don't account for a specific product's reinforcement.

Modified Bitumen Product Line

GAF's modified bitumen offerings include base ply and cap sheet products used in built-up and multi-ply assemblies, relevant on Spartanburg County's older mill and industrial buildings where a multi-ply system matches the building's roofing history or where an owner specifically wants the layered redundancy of a modified bitumen assembly over single-ply membrane. Installation method, whether torch-applied, cold-applied, or self-adhered, depends on the specific product line within GAF's modified bitumen catalog. We ask owners early in the process whether the building's occupancy or insurance carrier has any restriction on torch work, since that answer can steer the recommendation toward a cold-applied or self-adhered GAF product before a torch-applied system is even priced out.

FM and UL Assembly Listings

GAF maintains FM Approvals and UL listings for tested assemblies combining their membrane and insulation products in specific configurations. We check the applicable assembly number against a project's actual insulation, deck type, and fastening plan before finalizing a spec, since wind uplift and fire ratings apply to the tested assembly as a whole, not to the membrane product in isolation.

  • Confirm the current GAF product data sheet for the exact membrane or ply product specified
  • Match reinforcement type and thickness to the building's wind and hail exposure
  • Verify FM or UL assembly rating against actual insulation and fastening configuration
  • Review GAF's warranty terms and conditions before contract signing
  • Follow published torch safety protocol on any GAF modified bitumen torch-applied product
  • Keep safety data sheets and warranty documentation with the building file

Warranty Terms

GAF publishes material-only and system warranty options for their commercial roofing products, with term length and coverage scope varying by product category and installation requirements. We go through the specific warranty document applicable to the product being installed with the owner directly, since warranty language differs meaningfully between product lines and isn't interchangeable across GAF's broader catalog. That review includes checking whether the warranty tier requires a manufacturer-certified applicator or third-party inspection at completion, since some GAF warranty tiers carry documentation steps that need to be scheduled before tear-off begins rather than added on after the fact.

Regional Application: Hail and Humidity

Upstate South Carolina's hail exposure and humid summer thermal cycling inform how we weigh GAF's various membrane thickness and reinforcement options against each other for a given building. We use the manufacturer's own published test data on impact resistance and thermal performance rather than making general claims not supported by the actual product documentation. Buildings around downtown Spartanburg and the Morgan Square area with older mixed-use roof stock are a common place we bring GAF's product range into the conversation, since matching a smaller commercial building's budget with an appropriate warranty tier matters as much as picking the highest-spec membrane available.

Questions About GAF Commercial Products

Does GAF's commercial line overlap with their residential shingle products?

No, GAF's commercial roofing division produces distinct membrane and modified bitumen products separate from their residential asphalt shingle line, with their own spec sheets and warranty structures.

Is GAF TPO comparable to other manufacturers' TPO?

All TPO membranes share the same base chemistry, but specific formulations, reinforcement, and warranty terms vary by manufacturer. We compare published spec sheets directly rather than treating TPO as a single interchangeable product across brands.

Does GAF require specific installer certification for their warranties?

Warranty tier and coverage requirements, including any installer qualification needed for full-system coverage, are set by GAF and documented in their current warranty terms. We confirm applicable requirements for each specific project.

Can GAF modified bitumen be installed without a torch?

GAF offers cold-applied and self-adhered modified bitumen options within their broader product line, appropriate for buildings where torch application isn't suitable.

How do we confirm a GAF warranty covers our specific roof?

Warranty documentation issued at project completion specifies coverage terms for that installation. We provide that documentation to the owner as part of project closeout, along with the product data sheets for every membrane and accessory component actually installed on the roof.
